seoTitle: Loongson Processors Distributor | Desktop, Embedded, Server CPUs | LiTong
seoDescription: Authorized Loongson processor distributor. Supplying Loongson 3A5000, 3C5000, 2K1000, 2K2000 CPUs and development boards. Get selection guide and technical support.

faqs.question: What processor families does Loongson offer?
faqs.answer: Loongson offers three main processor families optimized for different applications. The Loongson 3 series targets high-performance desktop and server applications with multi-core configurations up to 16 cores, advanced cache hierarchies, and high memory bandwidth. The Loongson 2 series focuses on embedded and industrial applications, offering excellent performance-per-watt with integrated peripherals and extended temperature ranges. The Loongson 1 series serves low-power embedded markets with cost-effective single-core solutions for basic control applications. Each family is built on the LoongArch instruction set architecture and offers various performance tiers to match specific application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the processor families based on your application requirements. Contact our FAE team for detailed selection guidance matching your performance and power constraints.

faqs.question: How do I choose between Loongson desktop and embedded processors?
faqs.answer: Choosing between Loongson desktop and embedded processors depends on your application requirements. Desktop and server processors (Loongson 3 series) are ideal for applications requiring high compute performance, multiple cores, and extensive memory support. These processors excel in desktop computers, workstations, servers, and high-performance embedded systems. Embedded processors (Loongson 1 and 2 series) are optimized for power efficiency, cost sensitivity, and integration. They feature lower power consumption, integrated peripherals, and extended temperature ranges suitable for industrial control, network equipment, and IoT applications. Consider factors including compute requirements, power budget, operating environment, and cost targets when making your selection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your application requirements including performance needs, power constraints, and operating environment. Our FAE team can help match the optimal Loongson processor to your specifications.

faqs.question: What development tools are available for Loongson processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson provides comprehensive development tools and ecosystem support. Hardware development tools include evaluation boards, development kits, and reference designs for rapid prototyping. Software tools include GCC and LLVM compilers optimized for LoongArch, GDB debugger, and profiling tools. Loongson supports multiple operating systems including Loongnix (official Linux distribution), Kylin OS, UnionTech UOS, and various community Linux distributions. For embedded development, Loongson provides BSP (Board Support Package) packages with drivers and middleware. Documentation includes technical reference manuals, programming guides, and application notes. The company also offers technical training and certification programs for developers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Access Loongson development resources through our distribution channel. We can provide evaluation kits, technical documentation, and development support for your projects.

faqs.question: Are Loongson processors compatible with existing software?
faqs.answer: Loongson processors support software compatibility through multiple approaches. Native LoongArch applications deliver optimal performance and are the recommended approach for new development. For existing software, Loongson provides LAT (Loongson Architecture Translator) binary translation technology that enables running x86 and ARM applications on LoongArch processors with acceptable performance overhead. Major domestic software vendors have ported their applications to LoongArch native code, including office suites, databases, and industry-specific applications. The Linux ecosystem is well-supported with most open-source packages available for LoongArch. For specialized applications, software vendors can recompile their code using Loongson's toolchain with typically minimal modifications required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Assess your software requirements and compatibility needs. We can provide guidance on software migration strategies and ecosystem availability for your specific applications.

faqs.question: What is the price range for Loongson processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson processors are competitively priced compared to equivalent x86 and ARM solutions. Entry-level embedded processors (Loongson 1 series) are priced for cost-sensitive applications with prices comparable to ARM Cortex-A series processors. Mid-range embedded processors (Loongson 2 series) offer excellent performance-per-dollar for industrial and network applications. High-performance desktop and server processors (Loongson 3 series) provide competitive pricing for the performance delivered, typically 20-40% lower than equivalent x86 processors. Pricing varies based on volume, packaging, and temperature grade. As an authorized distributor, we offer competitive pricing and can provide quotations based on your specific requirements and volume forecasts.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for current pricing and volume discounts. We can provide competitive quotations and supply agreements for your production requirements.

categories.name: Desktop and Server Processors
categories.description: High-performance Loongson 3 series processors for desktop computers, workstations, and servers. These processors feature multi-core configurations, large cache hierarchies, and high memory bandwidth for demanding computing applications. Built on the LoongArch instruction set architecture, they provide competitive performance for general-purpose computing while maintaining complete IP independence.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Loongson Desktop and Server Processors
selectionGuide.description: Consider core count requirements, memory bandwidth needs, and thermal constraints when selecting Loongson 3 series processors.

faqs.question: What is the performance of Loongson 3A5000 compared to Intel and AMD processors?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3A5000 is a quad-core processor running at 2.3-2.5 GHz with competitive single-threaded performance comparable to mid-range x86 processors from previous generations. In multi-threaded workloads, the 3A5000 delivers performance suitable for desktop productivity applications, light server workloads, and development environments. While not matching the absolute performance of latest-generation Intel Core or AMD Ryzen processors, the 3A5000 offers adequate performance for typical office applications, web browsing, software development, and light content creation. The processor excels in performance-per-watt metrics and provides sufficient compute power for target applications while maintaining cost competitiveness.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate the 3A5000 for desktop and light server applications where absolute peak performance is not critical. Contact us for benchmark data and application-specific performance analysis.

faqs.question: How many cores do Loongson server processors support?
faqs.answer: Loongson server processors support various core configurations depending on the specific model. The Loongson 3C5000 series supports up to 16 cores per processor, designed for server and high-performance computing applications. Multi-socket configurations are supported for scaling to higher core counts in server platforms. The 3C5000 features large shared L3 cache (up to 32MB), high memory bandwidth with quad-channel DDR4 support, and advanced power management features. For entry-level server applications, the 3A5000 provides quad-core processing with dual-channel memory support. Loongson continues to develop higher core count processors for data center and enterprise applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select core count based on your application threading requirements and workload characteristics. Our FAE team can help determine optimal core configuration for your server applications.

faqs.question: What memory types are supported by Loongson desktop processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson desktop processors support DDR4 memory technology. The 3A5000 supports dual-channel DDR4-3200 memory, providing up to 51.2 GB/s memory bandwidth. Maximum memory capacity is typically 128GB or higher depending on the motherboard implementation. The memory controller supports ECC (Error Correction Code) for applications requiring data integrity. For server processors like the 3C5000, quad-channel DDR4 memory is supported for higher bandwidth requirements. Loongson processors use standard DDR4 DIMMs and SO-DIMMs, ensuring compatibility with widely available memory modules. Memory timing and configuration are handled through standard BIOS/UEFI settings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan your memory configuration based on bandwidth requirements and capacity needs. We can provide guidance on optimal memory configurations for your specific applications.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of Loongson desktop processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson desktop processors are designed with power efficiency as a key consideration. The 3A5000 has a TDP (Thermal Design Power) of 35-50W depending on operating frequency and workload, significantly lower than comparable x86 desktop processors. Typical power consumption under light loads is 10-15W, while full-load operation stays within the TDP envelope. The processors feature advanced power management including dynamic frequency scaling, core parking, and sleep states for idle power reduction. This power efficiency makes Loongson processors suitable for compact desktop systems, fanless designs, and applications where thermal management is challenging. The lower power consumption also translates to reduced electricity costs for large deployments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider Loongson processors for power-sensitive applications or compact system designs. We can provide thermal design guidance and power analysis for your specific use case.

faqs.question: What chipsets are available for Loongson desktop processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson desktop processors are supported by the 7A series chipsets providing comprehensive system functionality. The 7A2000 chipset offers integrated graphics, PCIe 3.0 connectivity, SATA, USB, and other I/O interfaces. For systems requiring discrete graphics or additional expansion, the chipset provides multiple PCIe lanes for GPU and add-in cards. The 7A1000 chipset provides basic I/O functionality for cost-sensitive applications. Loongson also offers bridge chips for specific applications including PCIe switches and SATA controllers. Reference designs and evaluation boards are available demonstrating typical chipset configurations. The chipset ecosystem continues to expand with new products offering enhanced features and performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select chipset based on your I/O requirements and graphics needs. We can provide reference designs and chipset selection guidance for your system design.

products.name: Loongson 3A5000 Quad-Core Desktop Processor
products.shortDescription: Quad-core LoongArch processor running at 2.3-2.5 GHz with 16MB L3 cache, dual-channel DDR4 support, and 35W TDP for desktop applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The Loongson 3A5000 is a high-performance quad-core processor based on the LoongArch instruction set architecture, designed for desktop and workstation applications.
Featuring four LA464 cores running at 2.3-2.5 GHz, 16MB shared L3 cache, and dual-channel DDR4-3200 memory support, the 3A5000 delivers competitive performance for general-purpose computing.
With a 35W TDP, integrated security features, and support for modern I/O interfaces, the 3A5000 is ideal for desktop computers, development workstations, and light server applications.
features: LoongArch instruction set architecture with complete IP independence
Quad-core LA464 superscalar out-of-order execution cores
16MB shared L3 cache for improved multi-threaded performance
Dual-channel DDR4-3200 memory controller with ECC support
Integrated security engine supporting domestic cryptography standards
PCIe 3.0 and SATA 3.0 interfaces for modern peripherals
Advanced power management with multiple sleep states
applications: Light server and edge computing applications

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Computing Platforms
faeReview.content: The 3A5000 represents a significant milestone in Loongson's processor development, offering genuine desktop-class performance on an independent architecture. In my experience supporting customer designs, the 3A5000 delivers consistent performance for office applications, development environments, and light server workloads. I particularly appreciate the low power consumption relative to the performance delivered, making it suitable for compact and fanless system designs. The integrated security features are valuable for government and enterprise applications requiring domestic cryptography. For software developers, the LoongArch toolchain has matured significantly, and most open-source software compiles without issues. I recommend the 3A5000 for customers seeking x86 alternatives for desktop and light server applications, particularly where technology independence is a priority.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent performance-per-watt for desktop applications with complete architecture independence.

alternativeParts.reason: Newer LA664 core architecture with improved IPC and performance
alternativeParts.useCase: Upgrade path for applications requiring higher single-threaded performance

alternativeParts.reason: Server-grade processor with significantly higher core count for parallel workloads
alternativeParts.useCase: Server and HPC applications requiring high thread count and memory bandwidth

companionParts.description: Companion chipset with integrated graphics and comprehensive I/O support
Development board with 3A5000 processor, memory, and standard interfaces
Dual-core embedded processor for I/O expansion and companion controller applications

faqs.question: What is the single-thread performance of the 3A5000?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3A5000 delivers competitive single-thread performance for its target market. The LA464 core features a superscalar out-of-order execution engine with advanced branch prediction and large instruction windows. In SPEC CPU benchmarks, the 3A5000 achieves results comparable to mid-range x86 processors from previous generations. For typical desktop applications including office productivity, web browsing, and software development, the 3A5000 provides responsive performance. The processor excels in integer workloads and delivers adequate floating-point performance for most applications. Real-world user experience is smooth for standard desktop tasks, and the processor can handle light content creation and compilation workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate the 3A5000 for applications where single-thread performance requirements are moderate. Contact us for benchmark data specific to your application type.

faqs.question: What operating systems are supported on the 3A5000?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3A5000 supports multiple operating systems optimized for the LoongArch architecture. Loongnix, the official Linux distribution from Loongson, provides the most complete support with optimized drivers and software packages. Kylin OS and UnionTech UOS are domestic Chinese operating systems with full 3A5000 support and extensive application compatibility. Various community Linux distributions including Debian, Fedora, and Gentoo have LoongArch ports available. The processor supports standard UEFI boot and ACPI power management for broad OS compatibility. For embedded applications, real-time operating systems such as RT-Thread are supported. Windows is not supported due to architecture differences.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose an operating system based on your application requirements and software ecosystem needs. We can provide guidance on OS selection and software compatibility.

faqs.question: Can the 3A5000 run x86 applications?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3A5000 can run x86 applications through binary translation technology called LAT (Loongson Architecture Translator). LAT translates x86 instructions to LoongArch instructions at runtime, allowing many x86 Linux applications to run without modification. Performance overhead varies by application type, typically ranging from 20-50% reduction compared to native execution. For best performance, applications should be compiled natively for LoongArch. Many popular open-source applications have been ported to LoongArch native code. Commercial software compatibility depends on vendor support for LoongArch. For critical applications, we recommend testing compatibility and performance with your specific software stack.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for native LoongArch application support where possible. Use binary translation for legacy software migration. Contact us for application compatibility testing.

faqs.question: What cooling solution is required for the 3A5000?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3A5000 with its 35W TDP can be cooled using various solutions depending on the system design and acoustic requirements. For standard desktop systems, a basic tower heatsink with 80-92mm fan provides adequate cooling with low noise levels. Compact systems can use low-profile heatsinks or heatpipe-based solutions. Fanless operation is possible with sufficiently large heatsinks and good chassis ventilation, making the 3A5000 suitable for silent PC builds and industrial applications. The processor features thermal monitoring and protection, automatically throttling frequency if temperature limits are exceeded. Reference thermal solutions are available from Loongson and ecosystem partners.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select cooling solution based on system size constraints and noise requirements. We can provide thermal design guidance and reference cooling solutions.

faqs.question: Is the 3A5000 suitable for server applications?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3A5000 can be used for light server and edge computing applications, though the 3C5000 is better suited for dedicated server deployments. The 3A5000's quad-core design and moderate memory bandwidth make it suitable for entry-level servers, development servers, and edge computing nodes. It works well for web serving, light database workloads, development environments, and containerized microservices. For high-density server applications, the limited core count and single-socket configuration may be constraints. The 3A5000 excels in scenarios requiring low power consumption and compact form factors. For production server deployments, consider the 3C5000 with its higher core count and multi-socket support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 3A5000 for light server and edge applications. Consider 3C5000 for high-performance server requirements. Contact us for server platform recommendations.

products.name: Loongson 3C5000 16-Core Server Processor
products.shortDescription: 16-core server processor running at 2.2 GHz with 32MB L3 cache, quad-channel DDR4 support, and 120W TDP for server and HPC applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The Loongson 3C5000 is a high-performance 16-core server processor designed for data center, cloud computing, and high-performance computing applications.
Featuring sixteen LA464 cores, 32MB shared L3 cache, and quad-channel DDR4 memory support, the 3C5000 delivers server-class performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
With support for multi-socket configurations, advanced virtualization features, and enterprise-grade reliability, the 3C5000 enables cost-effective server platforms for domestic cloud infrastructure.
features: 16 LA464 cores with simultaneous multithreading support
32MB shared L3 cache for high-throughput workloads
Quad-channel DDR4-3200 memory with ECC support
Multi-socket support for scaling to 64+ cores
Hardware virtualization acceleration with KVM support
PCIe 3.0 x40 lanes for high-speed I/O connectivity
Enterprise RAS features including memory scrubbing
applications: Data center and cloud computing servers

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Server Platforms
faeReview.content: The 3C5000 represents Loongson's entry into the server processor market with a compelling value proposition. In my work with data center customers, I've found the 3C5000 particularly attractive for cloud infrastructure deployments where cost efficiency and supply security are priorities. The 16-core configuration provides good thread density, and the quad-channel memory interface delivers adequate bandwidth for most server workloads. Multi-socket scaling works well for applications requiring higher core counts. I recommend ensuring your software stack is optimized for LoongArch to achieve best performance. The processor's power efficiency is notable for a server chip, helping reduce operational costs in large deployments. For customers building domestic cloud infrastructure, the 3C5000 provides a viable alternative to x86 server processors.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-effective server solution with good thread density for domestic cloud infrastructure.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower core count and power consumption for entry-level applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Development platforms and entry-level server applications with lower thread requirements

alternativeParts.reason: Lower power variant for power-constrained deployments
alternativeParts.useCase: High-density servers and power-optimized data center deployments

companionParts.description: Server chipset providing extended I/O and management features
Server reference platform with dual 3C5000 support
BMC processor for server management and out-of-band control

faqs.question: What server workloads are suitable for the 3C5000?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3C5000 is well-suited for various server workloads including web serving, application servers, virtualization hosts, and cloud infrastructure. The 16-core configuration handles multi-threaded applications effectively, making it suitable for Java application servers, PHP web stacks, and containerized microservices. Database workloads perform well when properly tuned for LoongArch. The processor excels in scale-out scenarios where multiple nodes work together. For compute-intensive HPC applications, performance depends on software optimization for the LoongArch architecture. The 3C5000 is particularly attractive for domestic cloud deployments, edge computing nodes, and applications where supply chain security is a priority.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate the 3C5000 for your specific server workload through benchmarking. We can provide reference platforms and performance analysis for your applications.

faqs.question: Does the 3C5000 support virtualization?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Loongson 3C5000 includes hardware virtualization acceleration features supporting KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine) virtualization. The processor provides nested page table support for efficient memory virtualization, reducing overhead for guest operating systems. Virtual CPU cores can be assigned to virtual machines with near-native performance for most workloads. The 16-core configuration allows hosting multiple virtual machines with adequate resources per instance. Memory virtualization is supported with hardware-assisted features. I/O virtualization is available through SR-IOV capable PCIe devices. Popular virtualization platforms including OpenStack and Proxmox have been ported to LoongArch. Container platforms such as Docker and Kubernetes run natively on the 3C5000.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 3C5000 is suitable for virtualization deployments. Plan your VM density based on workload requirements and perform testing with your specific applications.

faqs.question: What is the maximum memory capacity supported by the 3C5000?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3C5000 supports up to 256GB of DDR4 memory per socket through its quad-channel memory controller. The processor supports standard DDR4 RDIMMs and LRDIMMs for server applications. Memory speed support includes DDR4-3200 for optimal bandwidth. ECC (Error Correction Code) is supported for data integrity in server environments. In multi-socket configurations, each processor has its own memory channels, providing high aggregate memory bandwidth. Memory configuration flexibility allows balancing capacity and performance based on application requirements. For large memory deployments, consider the memory capacity limits of your chosen server platform and motherboard design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan memory configuration based on your application memory requirements. We can provide guidance on optimal memory sizing and configuration.

faqs.question: Can multiple 3C5000 processors work together in a system?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Loongson 3C5000 supports multi-socket configurations allowing multiple processors to work together in a single system. The processor includes high-speed interconnects for cache coherency and memory access between sockets. Dual-socket and quad-socket configurations are supported, enabling systems with up to 64 cores. In multi-socket systems, memory is distributed with each processor having local memory channels. NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access) awareness is important for optimal performance in multi-socket deployments. Operating systems and applications should be NUMA-aware to minimize remote memory access latency. Reference server platforms are available demonstrating multi-socket 3C5000 configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider multi-socket configurations for applications requiring high core counts. Ensure your software is NUMA-aware for optimal performance.

faqs.question: What is the performance per watt of the 3C5000 compared to x86 server processors?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 3C5000 offers competitive performance-per-watt metrics compared to x86 server processors in similar power envelopes. The 120W TDP for 16 cores provides approximately 7.5W per core, which is efficient for server workloads. In typical data center workloads, the 3C5000 delivers performance-per-watt comparable to entry-level x86 server processors. The efficiency advantage is more pronounced in integer workloads and web serving applications. For floating-point intensive workloads, the gap may be larger depending on optimization level. Power management features allow dynamic frequency scaling to optimize efficiency under varying loads. Overall, the 3C5000 provides a cost-effective and power-efficient option for scale-out server deployments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate total cost of ownership including power consumption for your deployment. The 3C5000 offers competitive efficiency for scale-out server applications.

categories.name: Embedded Processors
categories.description: Low-power Loongson 1 and 2 series processors for industrial control, network communication, and embedded computing applications. These processors offer excellent performance-per-watt with integrated peripherals, extended temperature ranges, and long-term availability for industrial applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Loongson Embedded Processors
selectionGuide.description: Consider power consumption, peripheral requirements, and operating temperature range when selecting embedded processors.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Loongson 1 and 2 series embedded processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson 1 series processors are single-core designs optimized for cost-sensitive and ultra-low-power applications. They feature basic processing capabilities with minimal power consumption, making them ideal for simple control tasks and IoT endpoints. Loongson 2 series processors are dual-core designs offering significantly higher performance for more demanding embedded applications. The 2K series includes integrated peripherals such as GMAC, SATA, USB, and PCIe, reducing system complexity and BOM cost. For applications requiring networking capabilities, industrial interfaces, or moderate computing power, the 2 series is recommended. The 1 series is suitable for basic control applications where cost and power are primary constraints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 1 series for basic control and cost-sensitive applications. Choose 2 series for networking, industrial control, and performance-critical embedded systems.

faqs.question: What integrated peripherals are available on Loongson 2K processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson 2K processors integrate comprehensive peripherals reducing the need for external components. The 2K1000 includes dual Gigabit Ethernet MACs, SATA 2.0 controller, USB 2.0 host and OTG, multiple UARTs, SPI, I2C, and GPIO. The 2K2000 adds PCIe 2.0 support, enhanced graphics capabilities, and additional USB ports. Both processors include memory controllers supporting DDR3 memory. The integrated GMAC supports network boot and industrial Ethernet protocols. SATA support enables direct storage connectivity without bridge chips. The rich peripheral set makes 2K processors suitable for network appliances, industrial controllers, and storage systems without requiring extensive external logic.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review peripheral requirements against 2K series specifications. We can provide detailed interface information and reference designs.

faqs.question: What temperature ranges do Loongson embedded processors support?
faqs.answer: Loongson embedded processors are available in multiple temperature grades to suit different application environments. Commercial grade supports 0°C to 70°C for standard indoor applications. Industrial grade supports -40°C to 85°C for harsh environments including outdoor installations and factory floors. Extended temperature options may be available for specific models. The processors feature temperature monitoring and thermal management capabilities. For automotive or specialized applications, contact us to discuss specific temperature requirements and qualification options. Industrial grade processors undergo additional testing and screening to ensure reliability across the full temperature range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select temperature grade based on your operating environment. Industrial grade is recommended for outdoor, factory, or harsh environment deployments.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of Loongson embedded processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson embedded processors are designed for power efficiency. The 1C101 single-core processor consumes less than 0.5W typical, making it suitable for battery-powered and energy-harvesting applications. The 2K1000 dual-core processor typically consumes 1-3W depending on workload and frequency. The 2K2000 with enhanced graphics and I/O consumes 3-5W typical. All processors support dynamic frequency scaling and power gating to reduce consumption during idle periods. Sleep modes enable sub-100mW operation for always-on applications. The low power consumption reduces thermal management requirements and enables fanless operation in most applications. Power consumption scales approximately linearly with CPU utilization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate power budget based on application duty cycle and performance requirements. We can provide detailed power analysis for your specific use case.

faqs.question: Are Loongson embedded processors suitable for real-time applications?
faqs.answer: Loongson embedded processors can be used for real-time applications with appropriate software configuration. The processors support real-time Linux variants including PREEMPT_RT patches for deterministic response times. While not hard real-time processors by design, the 1 and 2 series can achieve sub-millisecond interrupt latencies with proper kernel configuration. For applications requiring guaranteed response times, consider using the processors with real-time operating systems such as RT-Thread or SylixOS that have Loongson ports. The integrated peripherals include features supporting industrial protocols and deterministic I/O. For critical real-time requirements, evaluate the specific latency requirements against measured performance data.
faqs.decisionGuide: Assess your real-time requirements and evaluate with benchmark testing. We can provide guidance on real-time OS selection and configuration.

products.name: Loongson 2K1000 Dual-Core Embedded Processor
products.shortDescription: Dual-core GS264 embedded processor at 1.0 GHz with integrated GMAC, SATA, USB, and DDR3 support for industrial and network applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The Loongson 2K1000 is a highly integrated dual-core embedded processor designed for industrial control, network communication, and IoT gateway applications.
Featuring dual GS264 cores at 1.0 GHz, integrated Gigabit Ethernet, SATA, USB, and DDR3 memory controller, the 2K1000 provides a complete system-on-chip solution.
With industrial temperature support, low power consumption, and comprehensive peripheral integration, the 2K1000 reduces system complexity and BOM cost for embedded designs.
features: Dual GS264 superscalar cores with dual-issue pipeline
Integrated dual Gigabit Ethernet MACs with IEEE 1588 support
SATA 2.0 controller for direct storage connectivity
USB 2.0 host and OTG controllers
DDR3 memory controller with ECC support
Multiple UART, SPI, I2C, and CAN interfaces
Industrial temperature grade and low power consumption
applications: IoT gateways and edge computing nodes

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Embedded Systems
faeReview.content: The 2K1000 is one of my favorite embedded processors for industrial applications due to its exceptional integration level. The built-in dual GMAC eliminates the need for external Ethernet controllers, and the SATA interface enables direct SSD connection without bridge chips. I've successfully deployed 2K1000 in numerous industrial gateway and control applications. The industrial temperature grade and low power consumption make it reliable for harsh environments. The processor boots Linux quickly and the BSP support is comprehensive. For network applications, the hardware checksum offload and VLAN support are valuable features. I recommend the 2K1000 for any embedded application requiring networking and storage capabilities where minimizing external components is important.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent integration with dual GMAC and SATA reduces system complexity and cost.

alternativeParts.reason: Higher performance with enhanced graphics and additional I/O interfaces
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring higher performance, graphics output, or additional PCIe connectivity

alternativeParts.reason: Ultra-low power single-core for cost-sensitive basic control applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Simple control tasks, IoT endpoints, and battery-powered devices

companionParts.description: Evaluation board with 2K1000, DDR3, Ethernet, and expansion interfaces
Higher performance processor for companion host processing applications
Bridge chip for additional PCIe and I/O expansion

faqs.question: What is the boot time for Linux on the 2K1000?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 2K1000 can boot Linux to userspace in approximately 10-15 seconds with optimized configuration. Boot time depends on several factors including bootloader configuration, kernel size, and root filesystem type. Using U-Boot as bootloader and a minimal kernel with necessary drivers only can achieve faster boot times. Booting from SPI flash is typically faster than SATA storage. For applications requiring rapid boot, consider using initramfs or optimizing the startup sequence. The processor's integrated boot ROM supports multiple boot sources including SPI flash, NAND, and network boot. Real-world boot times in customer deployments typically range from 15-30 seconds depending on system complexity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Optimize boot configuration for your application requirements. We can provide guidance on fast boot techniques and bootloader configuration.

faqs.question: Does the 2K1000 support hardware encryption acceleration?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 2K1000 includes hardware acceleration for cryptographic operations supporting domestic Chinese cryptography standards. The processor features dedicated instructions for AES encryption and decryption, significantly improving throughput for encrypted communications and storage. SM3 and SM4 hash and encryption algorithms are also hardware-accelerated, important for compliance with Chinese security requirements. The cryptographic engine operates independently of the CPU cores, allowing concurrent processing. Performance for AES operations typically exceeds 1 Gbps, sufficient for full-rate Gigabit Ethernet encryption. Software support for hardware crypto is available in Linux kernel crypto API and OpenSSL through engine plugins.
faqs.decisionGuide: Leverage hardware crypto acceleration for secure communications and storage applications. We can provide software configuration guidance.

faqs.question: What industrial protocols are supported on the 2K1000?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 2K1000 supports various industrial protocols through hardware interfaces and software stacks. The integrated CAN controller supports CAN 2.0B protocol for industrial automation networks. Multiple UARTs with RS-485 support enable Modbus RTU and similar serial protocols. The Gigabit Ethernet MACs support standard industrial Ethernet protocols including EtherNet/IP, Profinet, and Modbus TCP through software implementations. SPI and I2C interfaces support sensor networks and device configuration. Real-time protocol support depends on software configuration and real-time kernel patches. For specific protocol requirements, reference implementations and third-party protocol stacks are available for Loongson platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify specific protocol requirements against available hardware interfaces and software support. We can provide reference implementations for common industrial protocols.

faqs.question: Can the 2K1000 drive a display directly?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 2K1000 has limited display capabilities compared to application processors. The processor includes a basic display controller supporting simple framebuffer output, suitable for configuration interfaces and basic HMI applications. Maximum resolution is typically limited to 1920x1080 at lower refresh rates. For applications requiring advanced graphics, video acceleration, or high-resolution displays, consider the 2K2000 which includes enhanced graphics capabilities. Alternatively, external GPU solutions can be connected via PCIe or USB. The 2K1000 is best suited for headless applications, simple character displays, or basic GUI requirements. For industrial HMI applications, many customers use external display controllers or connect to separate graphics subsystems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate display requirements carefully. Use 2K1000 for headless or simple display applications. Consider 2K2000 or external GPU for advanced graphics needs.

faqs.question: What is the long-term availability commitment for the 2K1000?
faqs.answer: Loongson provides long-term availability commitments for embedded processors to support industrial and infrastructure applications. The 2K1000 is guaranteed for availability of at least 10 years from product introduction, with typical availability extending 15+ years for successful products. This long-term availability is crucial for industrial equipment with extended product lifecycles. Loongson provides end-of-life notifications at least 12 months before discontinuation, with last-time-buy opportunities. Form, fit, and function compatible replacements are typically offered when products are discontinued. As an authorized distributor, we can provide formal long-term availability agreements and supply continuity planning for strategic customers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for long-term supply requirements when designing with 2K1000. Contact us for formal availability commitments and supply agreements.

products.name: Loongson 2K2000 Dual-Core Embedded Processor with Graphics
products.shortDescription: Dual-core GS264 embedded processor at 1.2 GHz with enhanced graphics, PCIe 2.0, dual GMAC, and comprehensive I/O for advanced embedded applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The Loongson 2K2000 is an advanced dual-core embedded processor featuring enhanced graphics capabilities and expanded I/O connectivity for sophisticated embedded systems.
With dual GS264 cores at 1.2 GHz, integrated 2D/3D graphics, PCIe 2.0, and comprehensive peripheral set, the 2K2000 addresses demanding HMI, gateway, and control applications.
The processor's enhanced graphics engine supports modern display requirements while the rich I/O set enables flexible system designs for industrial and commercial applications.
features: Dual GS264 cores with enhanced memory subsystem
Integrated 2D/3D graphics engine with OpenGL ES support
PCIe 2.0 x4 interface for expansion and GPU connectivity
Dual Gigabit Ethernet with advanced offload capabilities
Enhanced USB 3.0 and SATA 3.0 interfaces
DDR3-1066 memory controller with larger capacity support
Industrial temperature grade and enhanced reliability features
applications: Industrial HMI and panel PC applications
Advanced network gateways and security appliances

faeReview.author: Robert Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Solutions
faeReview.content: The 2K2000 addresses the gap between basic embedded processors and application processors, offering excellent graphics capabilities while maintaining industrial reliability. I've deployed 2K2000 in numerous HMI applications where the integrated graphics engine delivers smooth UI performance without requiring external GPU. The PCIe interface is valuable for adding custom I/O or high-speed connectivity. The enhanced Ethernet controllers with hardware offload improve network throughput significantly. Power consumption remains reasonable despite the enhanced features. The processor's reliability in industrial temperature ranges has been proven in factory floor deployments. I particularly appreciate the comprehensive BSP support that accelerates development. For any embedded application requiring graphics and advanced connectivity, the 2K2000 is my recommended choice.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent balance of graphics performance and industrial reliability for HMI applications.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost and power consumption for applications not requiring graphics or PCIe
alternativeParts.useCase: Headless embedded applications where graphics and high-speed expansion are not required

alternativeParts.reason: Significantly higher performance for compute-intensive applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring high processing power with embedded form factor

companionParts.description: Evaluation board with 2K2000, DDR3, HDMI output, and expansion connectors
PCIe bridge chip for additional expansion slots
Lower power alternative for cost-sensitive applications

faqs.question: What graphics capabilities does the 2K2000 provide?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 2K2000 includes an integrated 2D/3D graphics engine supporting modern display requirements. The graphics subsystem supports OpenGL ES 2.0 for 3D acceleration and provides hardware-accelerated 2D operations including blitting and composition. Maximum display resolution is 1920x1200 at 60Hz through HDMI or LVDS interfaces. The graphics engine can decode common video formats in hardware, reducing CPU load for multimedia applications. Multiple display outputs are supported for dual-screen configurations. Performance is suitable for industrial HMI, digital signage, and basic gaming applications. For comparison, the graphics performance is adequate for modern Linux desktop environments and browser-based applications with smooth scrolling and transitions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate graphics requirements against 2K2000 capabilities. Suitable for HMI and signage applications requiring moderate graphics performance.

faqs.question: Can the 2K2000 support dual independent displays?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Loongson 2K2000 supports dual independent display outputs, making it ideal for HMI applications requiring separate operator and public displays. The processor provides both HDMI and LVDS interfaces that can operate simultaneously with independent content. Each display can have different resolutions and refresh rates up to 1920x1200. The graphics driver supports extended desktop mode allowing applications to span both displays or run independently. Hardware cursor support is available on both outputs. For industrial panel PC applications, this enables local operator interface plus external monitor output. The dual display capability is fully supported in Linux with standard X11 or Wayland display servers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Leverage dual display capability for HMI applications. We can provide reference designs for dual-screen panel PC implementations.

faqs.question: What PCIe devices can be connected to the 2K2000?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 2K2000 provides a PCIe 2.0 x4 interface that can be configured as single x4, dual x2, or quad x1 lanes. This flexibility allows connecting various PCIe devices including additional Ethernet controllers for multi-port networking, WiFi and cellular modules for wireless connectivity, SATA controllers for additional storage, USB 3.0 controllers for enhanced USB connectivity, and FPGA modules for custom I/O expansion. The PCIe interface supports standard Linux drivers for most devices. For GPU expansion, the x4 interface can accommodate entry-level graphics cards for enhanced display capabilities. Reference designs demonstrate typical PCIe expansion configurations for network appliances and industrial controllers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan PCIe expansion based on system requirements. We can provide guidance on compatible PCIe devices and reference designs.

faqs.question: Is the 2K2000 suitable for digital signage applications?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 2K2000 is well-suited for digital signage applications requiring reliable long-term operation and moderate graphics capabilities. The integrated graphics engine supports smooth playback of HD video content and transitions for signage displays. The processor's industrial temperature range ensures reliable operation in various installation environments including outdoor enclosures. Low power consumption reduces thermal management requirements and enables fanless designs for silent operation. The dual display support allows for complex signage configurations. Linux-based signage software stacks are available for Loongson platforms. Long-term availability commitment ensures product lifecycle alignment with signage deployment timelines. Remote management capabilities through network interfaces support centralized content updates.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider 2K2000 for signage applications requiring reliability and moderate graphics. Evaluate specific content requirements against graphics capabilities.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 2K1000 and 2K2000 for network applications?
faqs.answer: Both 2K1000 and 2K2000 are suitable for network applications with key differences in performance and features. The 2K2000 offers higher CPU performance (1.2 GHz vs 1.0 GHz) beneficial for packet processing and encryption workloads. The 2K2000 adds PCIe 2.0 connectivity for additional network interfaces or hardware acceleration. USB 3.0 support in 2K2000 enables high-speed external storage or connectivity. Both processors include dual GMAC with similar Ethernet performance. For basic network gateways and routers, the 2K1000 provides adequate performance at lower cost. For advanced security appliances, high-throughput gateways, or applications requiring hardware crypto acceleration, the 2K2000 is recommended. Power consumption difference is modest (3W vs 5W) and typically not a deciding factor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 2K1000 for basic networking, 2K2000 for high-performance network appliances. We can provide performance benchmarks for specific network workloads.

categories.name: Development Boards and Modules
categories.description: Complete development platforms including evaluation boards, COM Express modules, and system-on-module solutions for rapid Loongson-based product development. These platforms provide reference designs and proven hardware for accelerating time to market.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Loongson Development Boards
selectionGuide.description: Consider processor requirements, interface needs, and form factor when selecting development platforms.

faqs.question: What types of development boards are available for Loongson processors?
faqs.answer: Loongson development platforms include several form factors to address different development needs. ATX and micro-ATX motherboards provide full-featured development platforms with extensive expansion options, suitable for desktop and server processor evaluation. Single-board computers (SBCs) integrate processor, memory, and I/O on a compact board for embedded application development. COM Express modules provide processor and memory on a standardized module for integration with custom carrier boards. System-on-module (SOM) solutions offer compact integration for space-constrained applications. Evaluation kits include the processor module plus reference carrier board and accessories. Reference designs demonstrate typical implementation approaches for specific applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select development platform based on your target product form factor and interface requirements. We can provide recommendations for specific application types.

faqs.question: What software is preloaded on Loongson development boards?
faqs.answer: Loongson development boards typically come with Linux-based operating systems preinstalled for immediate evaluation. The most common distribution is Loongnix, Loongson's official Linux distribution optimized for LoongArch processors. Alternative distributions including Kylin OS and UnionTech UOS may be available depending on the board variant. Development tools including GCC, GDB, and profiling tools are preinstalled. Documentation and example code are typically provided on the boot storage or accompanying media. Some boards may include trial versions of commercial software or development tools. The preloaded software provides a ready-to-use environment for evaluation and development without requiring additional setup time.
faqs.decisionGuide: Development boards are ready for immediate evaluation. We can provide guidance on software customization and alternative OS installation.

faqs.question: Can development boards be used in production products?
faqs.answer: Loongson development boards are primarily designed for evaluation and prototyping, but some can be adapted for low-volume production depending on the application. ATX and SBC form factor boards may be suitable for applications where the board size and I/O configuration match requirements. For volume production, custom carrier boards with COM Express or SOM modules are recommended for better integration and cost optimization. Development boards typically use commercial-grade components and may not meet specific industrial or reliability requirements for production. Long-term availability of development board SKUs may differ from production modules. We recommend transitioning from development boards to production-optimized designs for volume deployment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate development boards for production suitability based on volume and requirements. Contact us for production migration support and custom design services.

faqs.question: What technical support is provided with development boards?
faqs.answer: Loongson development boards include comprehensive technical support through the distribution channel. Hardware documentation includes schematics, PCB layout files, and bill of materials for reference designs. Software support includes BSP packages with drivers, kernel configurations, and bootloader sources. Application notes provide guidance on specific implementation topics. Technical support channels include email and phone support for board-specific questions. Access to online forums and knowledge bases provides community support. For complex issues, escalation to Loongson engineering is available through the distributor. Training materials and video tutorials may be provided for common tasks. Warranty support covers hardware defects according to standard terms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Leverage comprehensive support resources for your development. Contact our FAE team for technical assistance with development board usage.

faqs.question: Are carrier boards available for Loongson COM modules?
faqs.answer: Reference carrier boards are available for Loongson COM Express and SOM modules demonstrating typical integration approaches. These carrier boards provide standard PC-style interfaces including Ethernet, USB, SATA, and display outputs for evaluation purposes. Industrial carrier board designs with extended temperature components are available for industrial application development. Custom carrier board design services can be arranged for specific application requirements. Carrier board design files and documentation are provided to accelerate custom designs. Interface compatibility follows industry standards where applicable. For production deployment, most customers design custom carrier boards optimized for their specific I/O and form factor requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use reference carrier boards for initial evaluation. Plan custom carrier board design for production optimization. We can provide carrier board design services.

products.name: Loongson 3A5000 Development Board
products.shortDescription: ATX form factor development board with 3A5000 processor, DDR4 support, PCIe slots, and comprehensive I/O for desktop and server evaluation.
descriptionParagraphs: The 3A5000 Development Board provides a complete evaluation platform for the Loongson 3A5000 quad-core desktop processor in standard ATX form factor.
Featuring DDR4 memory slots, multiple PCIe expansion slots, SATA ports, USB 3.0, and Gigabit Ethernet, the board enables comprehensive processor evaluation.
The development board includes UEFI firmware, Linux preinstallation, and full documentation for rapid application development and benchmarking.

specifications.Interfaces: PCIe x16, PCIe x4, PCIe x1, USB 3.0, GbE

features: Standard ATX form factor for compatibility with PC cases
Dual DDR4-3200 DIMM slots supporting up to 64GB memory
PCIe 3.0 x16 slot for graphics card or accelerator installation
Multiple PCIe expansion slots for I/O expansion
M.2 NVMe slot for high-speed SSD storage
Comprehensive I/O including USB 3.0, SATA, and Ethernet
UEFI firmware with standard PC boot process

faeReview.author: James Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Platform Development
faeReview.content: The 3A5000 Development Board is an excellent platform for evaluating Loongson desktop processors and developing software. The standard ATX form factor makes it easy to work with using standard PC components and cases. I've used this board extensively for customer evaluations and software porting projects. The PCIe x16 slot allows testing with various graphics cards and accelerators. Memory compatibility has been good with major DDR4 module brands. The UEFI implementation is mature and supports standard boot methods. For customers transitioning from x86 platforms, this board provides a familiar development environment. I recommend this board as the starting point for any 3A5000-based development project.
faeReview.highlight: Standard ATX form factor with comprehensive expansion options for flexible evaluation.

alternativeParts.reason: Compact form factor for space-constrained applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Compact desktop and embedded applications requiring smaller footprint

alternativeParts.reason: Server-grade platform with higher core count and memory capacity
alternativeParts.useCase: Server evaluation and high-performance computing development

companionParts.description: Processor for custom board designs or upgrades
Chipset for custom motherboard designs
Qualified DDR4 memory kit for development board

faqs.question: What graphics cards are compatible with the 3A5000 Development Board?
faqs.answer: The 3A5000 Development Board's PCIe x16 slot supports AMD Radeon graphics cards with Loongson-compatible drivers. AMD RX 500 series and select RX 6000 series cards have been tested and validated. NVIDIA cards are not supported due to lack of LoongArch drivers. Integrated graphics solutions may be available depending on chipset configuration. For basic display output, many AMD cards work without additional configuration using standard Linux drivers. For GPU computing or advanced graphics, check specific card compatibility with Loongson support. Reference documentation lists tested and validated graphics cards. We recommend using validated cards for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select AMD Radeon graphics cards from the validated list. Contact us for current graphics card compatibility information.

faqs.question: Can the development board boot from NVMe SSD?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 3A5000 Development Board supports booting from NVMe SSDs connected to the M.2 slot. The UEFI firmware includes NVMe driver support for boot device recognition. NVMe boot provides significantly faster boot times and improved storage performance compared to SATA SSDs. Most mainstream NVMe SSDs are compatible, though validation with specific models is recommended. The M.2 slot supports PCIe 3.0 x4 for full NVMe performance. Both M.2 SATA and NVMe SSDs are supported, with NVMe preferred for performance. Boot priority can be configured in UEFI settings to select NVMe as the primary boot device.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NVMe SSD for best storage performance and fast boot. We can recommend validated NVMe SSD models for reliable operation.

faqs.question: What power supply is required for the development board?
faqs.answer: The 3A5000 Development Board uses standard ATX power supply connectors for broad compatibility. A 300W or higher 80 Plus certified power supply is recommended for typical configurations. The board uses 24-pin main power and 8-pin CPU power connectors standard on modern power supplies. Power consumption typically ranges from 50-100W depending on configuration and workload. Higher wattage power supplies (500W+) are recommended if using high-power graphics cards or multiple expansion cards. Standard ATX power supplies from major brands are compatible. The board includes power regulation and protection circuits for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use quality 300W+ ATX power supply for basic configurations. Select higher wattage for graphics card or expansion card installations.

faqs.question: Is the development board schematic available?
faqs.answer: Yes, schematic and design files are available for the 3A5000 Development Board to support customer reference designs and custom motherboard development. Documentation includes detailed schematics in PDF format, PCB layout files, and bill of materials. Design files demonstrate proper implementation of power delivery, memory interface, and I/O connectivity. Application notes provide guidance on design considerations and best practices. The documentation package is available to registered developers and customers under NDA. Reference designs can significantly accelerate custom motherboard development by providing proven implementation examples. Technical support is available for questions regarding reference design usage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Access reference design files for custom motherboard development. Contact us for documentation access and design support.

faqs.question: Can multiple development boards be networked for cluster testing?
faqs.answer: Multiple 3A5000 Development Boards can be networked together using the integrated Gigabit Ethernet for cluster and distributed computing testing. Standard Ethernet switches and cabling are used for interconnection. MPI (Message Passing Interface) and other clustering software is available for LoongArch platforms. The boards can run common cluster management tools including SLURM and Kubernetes. For high-performance interconnect testing, InfiniBand or high-speed Ethernet cards can be installed in PCIe slots. Cluster configurations are useful for testing parallel applications and evaluating scaling characteristics. Reference cluster configurations and setup guides are available for common use cases.
faqs.decisionGuide: Set up multiple boards for cluster testing using standard Ethernet networking. We can provide cluster configuration guidance and software support.

products.name: Loongson 2K1000 Evaluation Board
products.shortDescription: Compact evaluation board with 2K1000 processor, integrated peripherals, and expansion headers for embedded system development.
descriptionParagraphs: The 2K1000 Evaluation Board provides a comprehensive platform for evaluating and developing with the Loongson 2K1000 embedded processor.
Featuring the 2K1000 SoC with dual-core processor, integrated GMAC, SATA, USB, and industrial interfaces, the board enables rapid embedded system prototyping.
The compact form factor with standard expansion connectors makes it ideal for industrial control, network appliance, and IoT gateway development.

specifications.Interfaces: 2x GbE, SATA, USB, UART, SPI, I2C, GPIO

features: Integrated 2K1000 SoC with comprehensive peripheral set
1GB DDR3 memory soldered onboard for reliability
Dual Gigabit Ethernet ports with hardware offload
SATA connector for storage expansion
Rich serial interfaces including UART, SPI, I2C, CAN
Industrial temperature grade for harsh environments
Expansion headers for custom I/O and module connection

faeReview.author: Sarah Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Embedded Development
faeReview.content: The 2K1000 Evaluation Board is my go-to platform for customer embedded projects. The board's integration level significantly reduces prototyping time - everything needed for a complete system is included. I particularly appreciate the industrial temperature grade, which allows testing in real-world conditions. The dual Ethernet ports with hardware checksum offload are valuable for network appliance development. The expansion headers provide flexibility for connecting custom I/O. Linux BSP support is comprehensive with mainline kernel support. For customers new to Loongson, this board provides an excellent starting point with minimal additional components needed. I've used this board for industrial gateway, control system, and network appliance projects with great success.
faeReview.highlight: Highly integrated embedded platform with industrial temperature support for reliable prototyping.

alternativeParts.reason: Enhanced performance with graphics and PCIe for advanced applications
Compact module form factor for integration into custom designs
alternativeParts.useCase: Production designs requiring custom carrier board integration

companionParts.description: Processor for volume production designs
Reference carrier board design files for custom integration
Power adapter, cables, and accessories kit

faqs.question: What is the boot time for the 2K1000 Evaluation Board?
faqs.answer: The 2K1000 Evaluation Board typically boots Linux to userspace in 15-25 seconds depending on configuration. Boot from SPI flash is faster than SATA storage. Using a minimal root filesystem and optimized kernel can reduce boot time to under 15 seconds. U-Boot initialization takes approximately 5 seconds. The board supports boot from SPI flash, SATA, and network boot options. For applications requiring fast boot, consider using initramfs or optimizing the startup sequence. Real-time operating systems such as RT-Thread boot significantly faster, typically under 5 seconds. Boot time optimization techniques are documented in the BSP guide.
faqs.decisionGuide: Optimize boot configuration for your application requirements. We can provide guidance on fast boot implementation.

faqs.question: Can the evaluation board be powered by PoE?
faqs.answer: The standard 2K1000 Evaluation Board does not include Power over Ethernet (PoE) support. The board requires 12V DC input through a standard DC power jack. However, the dual Ethernet ports can be used with external PoE splitters to receive power over Ethernet. For custom designs requiring PoE, the 2K1000 reference design can be modified to include PoE interface circuitry. PoE capability is commonly requested for network appliance and IoT gateway applications. Third-party PoE splitter modules are compatible with the board's power input requirements. For volume production, custom carrier boards with integrated PoE can be designed based on the reference schematics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use external PoE splitter for PoE power, or design custom carrier with integrated PoE for production.

faqs.question: What real-time operating systems are supported on the 2K1000 Evaluation Board?
faqs.answer: The 2K1000 Evaluation Board supports multiple real-time operating systems optimized for embedded applications. RT-Thread is a popular domestic RTOS with comprehensive 2K1000 support and rich component ecosystem. SylixOS is another domestic real-time operating system with Loongson platform support. Real-time Linux with PREEMPT_RT patches provides deterministic response while maintaining Linux compatibility. FreeRTOS has been ported to Loongson platforms for lightweight applications. The choice of RTOS depends on application requirements including real-time constraints, middleware needs, and development team expertise. BSP packages and technical support are available for major RTOS options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select RTOS based on real-time requirements and software ecosystem needs. We can provide RTOS evaluation support and BSP packages.

faqs.question: Are expansion modules available for the evaluation board?
faqs.answer: Expansion modules are available for the 2K1000 Evaluation Board to add functionality and interface options. LCD display modules connect to the expansion headers for HMI development. CAN bus modules add additional CAN interfaces for industrial applications. Wireless modules including WiFi and 4G/5G cellular can be connected via USB or expansion interfaces. ADC/DAC modules add analog I/O capabilities. GPIO expansion modules increase digital I/O count. Third-party Arduino-compatible shields can be adapted for use with the board's expansion headers. Custom expansion modules can be designed using the expansion header pinout documentation. The expansion ecosystem continues to grow with community and commercial contributions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore available expansion modules for your application needs. We can provide expansion module recommendations and interface documentation.

faqs.question: Can the evaluation board be used for production deployment?
faqs.answer: The 2K1000 Evaluation Board is designed primarily for development and evaluation, but can be used for low-volume production in some cases. The board uses industrial-grade components suitable for industrial environments. However, the board form factor and I/O configuration may not match specific product requirements. For volume production, custom carrier boards optimized for the target application are recommended. The 2K1000 SOM module with custom carrier is the typical production path. Long-term availability of evaluation board SKUs may differ from production modules. Cost optimization is possible with custom designs. We recommend transitioning from evaluation boards to production-optimized designs for deployments exceeding 100 units annually.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use evaluation board for prototyping and low-volume production. Plan custom design for volume deployment. We can provide production migration support.

categories.name: Chipsets and Bridge Chips
categories.description: Companion chipsets and bridge chips providing comprehensive system solutions for Loongson processors. These devices include memory controllers, PCIe bridges, SATA controllers, and I/O expansion chips to build complete system solutions.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Loongson Chipsets and Bridge Chips
selectionGuide.description: Consider interface requirements, expansion needs, and system architecture when selecting companion chips.

faqs.question: What functions do Loongson chipsets provide?
faqs.answer: Loongson chipsets provide essential system functions to complement the main processors. The 7A series chipsets provide integrated graphics, memory control, and comprehensive I/O interfaces for desktop and server platforms. Bridge chips including PCIe switches enable system expansion and connectivity. SATA controllers provide storage interface expansion. USB controllers add additional USB connectivity. Ethernet PHYs and switches provide network interface options. These companion chips enable flexible system designs while reducing external component count. Reference designs demonstrate typical chipset configurations for various applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select chipsets based on your system I/O requirements and expansion needs. We can provide chipset configuration guidance.

faqs.question: How do I design a motherboard with Loongson chipsets?
faqs.answer: Designing a motherboard with Loongson chipsets requires understanding the processor and chipset interfaces. Reference designs and schematics are available for typical configurations. Key design considerations include power delivery for processor and chipset, DDR memory interface routing and termination, PCIe trace length matching and impedance control, and clock distribution for synchronous interfaces. Signal integrity analysis is recommended for high-speed interfaces. Thermal design must accommodate chipset power dissipation. Layout guidelines are provided in hardware design manuals. For complex designs, consider engaging with Loongson's design support services. Prototype testing with reference boards is recommended before custom design commitment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with reference designs and follow hardware design guidelines. We can provide design review services and technical support.

faqs.question: What PCIe expansion options are available with Loongson chipsets?
faqs.answer: Loongson platforms support PCIe expansion through processor-integrated controllers and bridge chips. The 3A5000 processor provides PCIe 3.0 lanes for graphics and expansion. The 7A2000 chipset provides additional PCIe lanes for I/O expansion. PCIe switch chips enable fan-out to multiple slots or devices. Typical configurations support x16 for graphics, x4 for NVMe storage, and multiple x1 lanes for peripheral expansion. PCIe 2.0 and 3.0 devices are supported depending on the specific interface. Hot-plug support depends on system implementation. Reference designs demonstrate PCIe topology options for different system requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan PCIe topology based on expansion requirements. We can provide PCIe configuration recommendations and reference designs.

faqs.question: Are Loongson chipsets available in industrial temperature grades?
faqs.answer: Selected Loongson chipsets are available in industrial temperature grades for harsh environment applications. The 7A1000 chipset is available in -40°C to +85°C industrial grade for industrial motherboard designs. Bridge chips and controllers may have industrial grade options depending on the specific device. Industrial grade devices undergo additional testing and screening to ensure reliability across the temperature range. For automotive or specialized applications, discuss specific temperature requirements with our technical team. Lead times for industrial grade components may be longer than commercial grade. Volume commitments may be required for industrial grade procurement.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify industrial grade for harsh environment applications. Contact us for industrial grade availability and procurement options.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of Loongson chipsets?
faqs.answer: Loongson chipset power consumption varies by device and configuration. The 7A2000 desktop chipset typically consumes 5-10W depending on active interfaces and graphics load. The 7A1000 embedded chipset consumes 2-5W in typical applications. Bridge chips and controllers have lower power consumption, typically 1-3W. Power management features allow dynamic power reduction when interfaces are idle. Thermal design should accommodate maximum power dissipation under worst-case conditions. Power consumption scales with the number of active interfaces and their operating speeds. Reference designs include power analysis for typical configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider chipset power in system thermal and power supply design. We can provide power analysis for your specific configuration.

products.name: Loongson 7A2000 Desktop Chipset
products.shortDescription: Comprehensive desktop chipset with integrated graphics, DDR4 memory controller, PCIe 3.0, SATA, and USB for 3A5000 processor platforms.
descriptionParagraphs: The Loongson 7A2000 is a feature-rich desktop chipset designed to complement the 3A5000 processor in desktop and workstation platforms.
Integrating graphics engine, DDR4 memory controller, PCIe 3.0, SATA 3.0, and USB 3.0, the 7A2000 provides a complete platform solution.
The chipset enables cost-effective desktop motherboard designs while providing modern I/O connectivity and expansion options.
features: Integrated graphics engine with HDMI and VGA output
DDR4 memory controller supporting dual channel operation
PCIe 3.0 x16 and multiple x1 lanes for expansion
SATA 3.0 controller for storage connectivity
USB 3.0 and USB 2.0 host controllers
HD Audio interface for audio output
LPC and SMBus for legacy and management interfaces

faeReview.author: Kevin Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Platform Architecture
faeReview.content: The 7A2000 chipset completes the Loongson desktop platform by providing essential I/O and graphics capabilities. In my experience with motherboard designs, the 7A2000 integration level significantly reduces BOM cost and design complexity compared to discrete implementations. The integrated graphics are adequate for desktop productivity applications and basic media playback. PCIe connectivity supports modern expansion cards including NVMe SSDs and graphics cards. The chipset's power consumption is reasonable for desktop applications. Driver support in Linux is mature and stable. For customers designing custom motherboards, the 7A2000 reference design provides an excellent starting point. I recommend this chipset for any 3A5000-based desktop or workstation design.
faeReview.highlight: High integration level reduces BOM cost and simplifies desktop motherboard design.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower power and cost for embedded applications with reduced I/O requirements
alternativeParts.useCase: Embedded and industrial applications not requiring advanced graphics

alternativeParts.reason: Enhanced graphics and I/O for premium desktop platforms

companionParts.description: Desktop processor for 7A2000-based platforms
Reference design demonstrating 7A2000 integration
Qualified DDR4 memory for 7A2000 platforms

faqs.question: What graphics capabilities does the 7A2000 provide?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 7A2000 integrates a 2D/3D graphics engine suitable for desktop productivity and basic multimedia applications. The graphics subsystem supports 2D acceleration for desktop composition and window management. 3D graphics support includes OpenGL ES 2.0 for basic 3D applications. Maximum display resolution is 1920x1200 at 60Hz through HDMI or VGA interfaces. Dual independent display output is supported for extended desktop configurations. Video playback acceleration is provided for common formats including H.264. Graphics performance is comparable to entry-level integrated graphics in other desktop platforms. For gaming or professional graphics applications, discrete graphics cards can be added via the PCIe x16 slot.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate graphics requirements for your application. Integrated graphics are suitable for productivity; add discrete GPU for advanced graphics needs.

faqs.question: How many PCIe lanes does the 7A2000 provide?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 7A2000 provides flexible PCIe lane configuration to support various expansion options. The chipset provides up to 20 PCIe 3.0 lanes that can be configured as x16 for graphics plus x4 for storage, or multiple x4 and x1 configurations for various expansion cards. The processor provides additional PCIe lanes for direct device connection. Total system PCIe bandwidth supports high-speed NVMe storage and graphics card simultaneous operation. PCIe lane bifurcation allows flexible slot configurations. Reference designs demonstrate typical PCIe topologies for desktop and workstation applications. The PCIe implementation is compatible with standard PCIe devices including graphics cards, network cards, and storage controllers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan PCIe configuration based on expansion requirements. We can provide PCIe topology recommendations for your system design.

faqs.question: Does the 7A2000 support modern storage interfaces?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Loongson 7A2000 supports modern storage interfaces for high-performance storage solutions. The chipset includes SATA 3.0 controllers supporting 6Gbps data rates for SSD and hard drive connectivity. Multiple SATA ports enable storage expansion and RAID configurations. NVMe storage is supported through PCIe M.2 slots connected to processor or chipset PCIe lanes. USB 3.0 ports support external storage devices at high speeds. The storage subsystem provides performance comparable to other modern desktop platforms. TRIM support is available for SSD optimization. Hot-plug support depends on system implementation and software configuration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SATA for cost-effective storage, NVMe for maximum performance. We can provide storage configuration guidance.

faqs.question: What audio capabilities does the 7A2000 provide?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 7A2000 includes HD Audio interface support for modern audio solutions. The chipset provides the controller interface for connecting external HD Audio codecs. Multi-channel audio output is supported depending on the codec implementation. Typical implementations support 5.1 or 7.1 channel analog output. Digital audio output is available through HDMI for home theater applications. Audio quality depends on the external codec and analog circuit design. Microphone input and line-in are supported for recording applications. Linux ALSA driver support is available for the HD Audio interface. Reference designs include proven audio codec implementations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select audio codec based on audio quality requirements. Reference designs provide proven implementations.

faqs.question: Is the 7A2000 suitable for industrial applications?
faqs.answer: The Loongson 7A2000 is primarily designed for commercial desktop applications with 0°C to 70°C operating temperature. For industrial applications, the 7A1000 embedded chipset with industrial temperature grade is recommended. Industrial applications requiring 7A2000 features may consider custom thermal management solutions.
